response:
```python
# test_fizzbuzz.py
import pytest

from your_module import fizzbuzz  # Replace "your_module" with the actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("n", "expected"),
    [
        (1, "1"),
        (2, "2"),
        (3, "Fizz"),
        (6, "Fizz"),
        (5, "Buzz"),
        (10, "Buzz"),
        (15, "FizzBuzz"),
        (30, "FizzBuzz"),
    ],
)
def test_fizzbuzz_happy_path(n, expected):
    assert fizzbuzz(n) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("n", "expected"),
    [
        (0, "FizzBuzz"),
        (-1, "-1"),
        (-3, "Fizz"),
        (-5, "Buzz"),
        (-15, "FizzBuzz"),
    ],
)
def test_fizzbuzz_edge_cases(n, expected):
    assert fizzbuzz(n) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("n", "expected"),
    [
        (1_000_000, "Buzz"),
        (999_999, "Fizz"),
        (15_000_000, "FizzBuzz"),
        (1_000_001, "1000001"),
    ],
)
def test_fizzbuzz_large_numbers(n, expected):
    assert fizzbuzz(n) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "bad_input",
    [
        None,
        "15",
        [],
        {},
        object(),
    ],
)
def test_fizzbuzz_invalid_inputs_raise_type_error(bad_input):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        fizzbuzz(bad_input)
```
